title: Pungent Thai Chicken Skewers
description: Crispy chicken skewers with a pungent flavor, perfect for a monsoon Thai dish. This diabetic-friendly recipe uses buttermilk and a rich deglaze-fond to add depth to the dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine chicken breast, buttermilk, garlic, ginger, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and red pepper flakes. Mix well to coat the chicken.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with coconut oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the chicken skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they are cooked through and slightly charred.
While the chicken is grilling, combine the deglaze-fond, coconut oil, and fish sauce in a small saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the grill and brush with the deglaze-fond sauce.
Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves and serve immediately.
Let the skewers rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
